title: acianthus pusillus
intro: Acianthus pusillus, also known as the Small Fringed Fairy Orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family. The plant is native to New Zealand and is often found growing in wetlands and rocky areas. The leaves and roots of the Acianthus pusillus have been used in traditional medicine for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas or infusions to promote wound healing, reduce swelling, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and congestion.
